sections.paragraph:
ἀφανισμός, οῦ, ὁ the condition of being no longer visible, freq. in the transferred sense destruction (s. ἀφανίζω; Polyb. et al.; Diod S 15, 48, 1; Herm. Wr. p. 364 Sc.; LXX; En 12:4; ParJer 3:13; 4:10; Jos., Ant. 1, 70) ἐγγὺς ἀφανισμοῦ is near destruction Hb 8:13.—DELG s.v. φαίνω. M-M.

freq. freq. = frequent(ly)

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Herm. Wr. Herm. Wr. = Hermetic Writings—List 5

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

En En = I Enoch—List 2

ParJer ParJer = Paraleipomena Jeremiou, II? a.d.—List 2

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 155
